results.classification_summary: The Adaptation Benefit Mechanism (ABM) is an innovative mechanism for mobilizing new and additional public and private sector finance for enhanced climate change adaptation action in Africa. results.proposal_summary: The African Development Bank's Adaptation Benefit Mechanism (ABM) is designed to mobilize public and private finance for climate change adaptation action in Africa. results.proposal_summary: The UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) is commissioning a research study to provide an in-depth analysis of start-up funding in East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Ethiopia). The study aims to map the start-up funding landscape, understand funding sources and barriers to scaling. The expected funding is between £100,001 to £500,000. Eligible organizations include NGOs, UK-based non-profit organizations, educational institutions, humanitarian relief organizations, and organizations based in Low to Middle Income Countries (LMIC). The deadline for submissions was October 3, 2025. Collaboration between start-up ecosystem practitioners and established academic experts is encouraged. Co-financing details are not mentioned.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on start-up funding and private sector development in East Africa relates to ILO's work on employment policy, job creation, and sustainable enterprises.

results.classification_summary: The Strengthening Humanitarian Assistance and Response to Protection Risks in Ukraine project (SHARP) by the FCDO is a closed call for proposals aimed at providing inclusive life-saving protection and assistance services for civilians affected by the conflict in Ukraine. results.title: RISE Grants Challenge 2025
results.organization: International Union for Conservation of Nature
eligible_activities: Projects including a gender analysis to assess context and inform gender-based violence risk mitigation approaches
Projects including a baseline and endline assessment to measure changes in perceptions of safety and other relevant RISE indicators
Project coordinating with existing mechanisms to address gender-based violence, such as existing referral pathways and service providers
Projects engaging men and boys as a risk mitigation strategy in complement to any women’s economic empowerment and other capacity building and empowerment activities
Evidence-based strategies adapted from global GBV approaches—tailored to environment-focused sectors—to contribute to cross-sectoral learning and strengthen integration in RISE-eligible countries
Integration of risk mitigation measures, including strengthening safeguards in environmental programmes and training environmental stakeholders
Strategies, tools and capacities contributing to the overall global knowledge and capacity base to address GBV and environment linkages
Institutional learning to improve implementation and scale-up of rights-based gender-responsive environment approaches and outcomes

Innovative arrangements – e.g., via partnerships, institutional enabling conditions, policy reform or social protection measures – that contribute to existing evidence on addressing gender-based violence in environment linkages

Enabling conditions to implement interventions to reduce gender-based violence and support survivors, for example through legislative action, infrastructure or institutionalisation, or mobilising political will

Bridging knowledge gaps, for example through deepening local and contextual knowledge; showcasing context-specific promising practices; and/ or demonstrating how RISE grantees’ interventions will influence national, regional or global policy and programming agendas
results.proposal_summary: The IUCN RISE grants challenge aims to fund projects addressing gender-based violence within environment and climate-related sectors, focusing on risk mitigation around Indigenous-managed lands and protected areas. Open to diverse organizations in eligible countries, it requires partnerships between environmental and GBV-expert groups. The deadline for applications is September 15, 2025. The projects should be aligned with thematic responsiveness, address the scope, purpose and objectives of the RISE grants challenge, and meet the programmatic requirements. Although not explicitly mentioned, co-financing is not likely required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on gender equality and addressing gender-based violence, which indirectly links to the ILO's work on equality and decent work, particularly in supply chains due to environmental factors.

results.title: Thematic Call 2019/2
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
results.proposal_summary: The International Climate Initiative (IKI) is offering funding from 5 million to 20 million EUR per project through its Thematic Call 2019/2. The call covers a range of thematic priorities, including implementation of the Paris Agreement goals, capacity building for transparency, digitalization of transportation, alternatives to fossil energy infrastructure, strategic use of cooperative approaches, climate-friendly product standards, community-based adaptation, ecosystem restoration, peatland conservation, biodiversity protection, pollinator diversity, and capacity building for the World Biodiversity Council. The call targets countries in Asia, Latin America, and Africa. Eligible organizations were invited to submit project outlines between November 7, 2019, and April 2, 2020. Co-financing requirements and specific eligibility criteria are not mentioned in this summary.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call addresses themes indirectly related to ILO's mandate, such as sustainable economies and societies, though it primarily focuses on environmental and climate-related initiatives.

theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
eligible_activities: Strengthened global and national frameworks to stop plastic pollution and marine litter
Reduced consumption and demand for plastic
Improved plastic waste management in partner countries
Increased resource mobilization and safe job creation in the private sector within the circular economy
Reduced health and environmental damage from plastic pollution and marine litter
results.proposal_summary: The Norwegian Agency for Development Cooperation (Norad) is seeking proposals for projects aimed at reducing plastic pollution and marine litter in developing countries, as part of the second phase of the Norwegian development program. Priority countries include Indonesia, Vietnam, Kenya, Ghana, and Tanzania. This call is open to invited non-governmental organizations as lead applicants; consortia applications are encouraged. There's no specific deadline mentioned, it is ongoing. The program aims to strengthen frameworks, reduce consumption, improve waste management, increase resource mobilization, and reduce health and environmental damage related to plastic pollution.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the CfP focuses on environmental issues (plastic pollution), it has a tangential link to ILO's mandate through potential job creation in the circular economy and improved working conditions related to waste management, relevant to just transition.

results.proposal_summary: Norad announces a call for proposals with 100 million NOK in funding for private sector development in Ukraine for 2026 and 2027. Open to applicants from all countries, the call aims to promote sustainable and investment-ready projects in sectors like energy production, food security, agriculture, and reconstruction of critical infrastructure. The scheme seeks to mobilize private capital, create jobs, and improve the framework conditions for the private sector in Ukraine. Applications must be submitted via the digital application form by June 17, 2026. Co-financing does not appear to be mandatory.

results.title: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives
theme: Inclusive governance, including diversity, democracy, human rights, 2SLGBTQI+ rights and the rule of law.
Gender equality and the empowerment of women and girls.
Peace and security, with a focus on conflict prevention and building peace.

organization_type: Government agencies at the municipal level
Government agencies at the regional level
Government agencies at the national level
results.proposal_summary: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI) provides funding for small, high-impact projects in developing countries. The fund supports projects led by local civil society organizations and focuses on areas such as inclusive governance, gender equality, peace and security, environmental action, and human dignity. Projects average around $31,000 each. Eligible applicants include local and international NGOs, academic institutions, government agencies, and Canadian NGOs. The deadline varies by country, with multiple deadlines listed in April and May 2026. Co-financing may be applicable but is not mentioned as a requirement.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The fund supports projects related to gender equality, fair jobs, and investing in the most vulnerable, which aligns with ILO's mandate related to decent work and social protection.

results.exclusion_reason: Deadline has passed.
results.classification_summary: This is a call for concept notes to fund projects in Afghanistan that delivers basic needs services specifically for women and girls, concerning the themes of gender-responsive health services, demand-driven skills training, and inclusive services that protect and advance the rights and empowerment of all Afghans. Funding is from Global Affairs Canada with CAD$2,000,000 to CAD$10,000,000 per project or none, for a duration of up to four years. Eligible applicants must be legally incorporated, not a sovereign entity or multilateral institution, and registered in Partners@International and provide separate financial statements for the two most recent fiscal years. The submission deadline was April 16, 2025.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The call specifies eligible organizations, and ILO as an international organization is not among them. It states: 'You are not a sovereign entity (a government of a country) or multilateral institution.'

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call refers to funding agreements and contributions, indicating a grant instrument.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The grant size is between CAD$2,000,000 and CAD$10,000,000, which is above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The submission deadline was April 16, 2025, which is in the past.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The call is for project funding to address specific needs, not for procurement of goods/services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The applicant organization must provide at least 5% of the total eligible direct project costs over the life of the project in cash and/or in-kind.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: There is no mention of a specific accreditation required.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: There are no rules stating that any consortium must be led by a non-UN entity.

results.title: Health, education and women's and girls’ rights and empowerment in Afghanistan

application_process.description: Funding decision and notification of applicants

eligible_activities: Inclusive services that protect and advance the rights and empowerment of all Afghans
Projects that align with the Women’s Voice and Leadership Program
Projects that contribute to Canada’s 10-Year Commitment to Global Health and Rights
funding_restrictions: Projects must avoid legitimizing the Taliban de facto authorities in any way.

Canada will not support projects that invest in or strengthen the Taliban de facto authorities’ ability or capacity to implement systems and institutions that are not inclusive, do not respect human rights, and/or that do not align with widely accepted standards for the principled delivery of international assistance.
Funding must not directly support de facto ministries or their institutions.
No construction or improvements to facilities controlled by the Taliban de facto authorities will be permitted.
results.proposal_summary: Global Affairs Canada is offering funding for projects that support the delivery of basic needs services to Afghans, especially women and girls, focusing on health, education, and empowerment. The projects should range from CAD$2,000,000 to CAD$10,000,000 and last up to four years. Eligible organizations must be legally incorporated and registered in Partners@International. The deadline for submitting a concept note was April 16, 2025. A minimum 5% co-financing is required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on skills training, women's empowerment, and equality, which aligns with ILO's mandate on employment, decent work, and gender equality.

results.exclusion_reason: Deadline passed, and applicant eligibility restricted to Canadian organizations, excluding the ILO as an international organization.
results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals from the Government of Canada (Global Affairs Canada) for a Skills Development and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) fund. The fund will provide sub-grants to organizations to support skills development and access to decent work for young people in ODA-eligible countries, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa. The total funding available is up to $49.5 million over 5 years. The deadline for submitting a proposal was March 6, 2025.  Eligible applicants are restricted to Canadian organizations, which excludes the ILO. Furthermore, the deadline has passed.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The call is open to Canadian organizations only. Therefore the ILO (an International Organization) is not eligible.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call is for the establishment and management of a fund that will provide sub-grants; this is a grant instrument.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The total funding available is up to $49.5 million.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline for submitting a proposal was March 6, 2025, which is in the past.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The (co)signatory organization(s) will provide at least 5% of the total eligible direct project costs over the life of the project in cash and/or in-kind

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: Your organization is a Canadian organization, which means for the purpose of this call that your organization is legally incorporated in Canada, established with an office and employees in Canada and can provide proof of legal status (e.g. a letter of incorporation) along with a Canada Revenue Agency business number.

results.title: Skills development and Technical and vocational education and training fund – Call for proposals
eligible_activities: planning and implementing effective skills development/TVET programming in Official Development Assistance-eligible countries
implementing gender transformative skills development/TVET, education and/or youth employment/entrepreneurship programming internationally
establishing partnerships with the private and/or philanthropic sector to achieve progress towards the FIAP.
funding_restrictions: the manager/s of the fund will not be eligible to receive funding from the fund to implement projects
results.proposal_summary: Global Affairs Canada is offering a call for proposals for a skills development and TVET fund, with a total funding of up to $49.5 million over 5 years. The fund aims to improve employment outcomes for young people in ODA-eligible countries, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by providing sub-grants to Canadian, international, and local organizations. Projects must align with Canada’s Feminist International Assistance Policy and contribute to increased employability skills and provision of quality TVET. The signatory organization(s) will provide at least 5% of the total eligible direct project costs. Applicants must be Canadian organizations with experience in managing grant-making funds and implementing TVET programs. The application deadline was March 6, 2025.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses on skills development and TVET programming, which aligns with the ILO's mandate to promote decent work through skills and employment policies.

results.proposal_summary: The European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF) 2021-2027 provides investment grants for aquaculture in Finland, from January 1, 2026, to December 31, 2027. It aims to finance sustainable aquaculture growth, improve social and ecological sustainability, and enhance business profitability. Specific themes include increasing value, improving quality, sustainable growth, digitalisation, energy efficiency, reducing environmental impacts, climate change adaptation, diversification, animal welfare, and worker safety. Only applications in Finnish or Swedish are accepted.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on investments in safety and improving working conditions in aquaculture aligns to a small extent with the ILO's mandate of Decent Work.

results.exclusion_reason: The ILO is not an eligible applicant because the application must be submitted in Finnish or Swedish.
results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals under the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF) 2021-2027, focusing on aquaculture innovations, development, and advice in Finland. The call offers project grants to companies and non-profit organizations for research, development, and innovation projects (RDI) related to aquaculture. There are two application tracks: continuous application for for-profit RDI projects and time-limited application periods for non-profit RDI projects. A deadline for the time-limited non-profit applications will stretch until 2027-12-31. The maximum funding available is EUR 500,000 for non-profit RDI projects, with at least EUR 200,000 allocated to projects under the fish welfare theme. Eligible applicants must apply in Finnish or Swedish.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The application must be submitted in Finnish or Swedish meaning an international organisation would not be eligible.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: Up to EUR 500,000 may be granted through this procedure

Deadline is future.evidence: Application period ends on 31 December 2027 at 23:59

results.proposal_summary: The US Department of State, Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights, and Labor (DRL) is offering grants between USD 500,000 and USD 3,000,000 for programs supporting Internet Freedom in countries in the State Department-defined regions of Africa, East Asia and the Pacific, North Africa and the Middle East, South and Central Asia, and the Western Hemisphere. Eligible applicants are U.S.- or foreign-based non-profit/non-governmental organizations (NGOs), public international organizations, private/public/state institutions of higher education, or for-profit organizations or businesses. results.title: Translation and Contextualization of PAFA’s Quality Assurance Review (QAR) Guidance for Francophone African PAOs and Regulators
eligible_activities: Technically accurate translation of the QAR Guidance from English to French.
Contextualise the translated document for Francophone Africa PAOs and regulators, including alignment with OHADA, national legislation, and professional oversight systems.
Maintain consistency with international auditing standards and IFAC SMO requirements.
Develop stakeholder validation mechanisms to ensure practical applicability.
Deliver a final publication-ready French QAR Guidance in MS Word and PDF formats.
results.proposal_summary: The African Development Bank seeks consultancy services for translating and contextualizing the PAFA Quality Assurance Review (QAR) Guidance for Francophone African PAOs and regulators. The consultant will translate, contextualize, and develop validation mechanisms. This is a procurement opportunity, not a grant. All African countries are listed and some non-regional member countries are indicated.

results.title: Financing CSO Projects
results.proposal_summary: The Agence Française de Développement (AFD) provides funding for Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) through various mechanisms, including the CSO Initiatives Facility (co-financing projects), Crisis and Post-Crisis Calls for Projects (APCC), and the Support Fund for Feminist Organizations (FSOF). The eligible countries are those eligible for official development assistance according to the OECD's DAC list. The funding amounts and specifics vary based on the program, with some requiring a minimum project amount. The CSO Initiatives Facility requires French associations, syndicates, foundations, or local law organizations that have been financed at least once by AFD to apply. The earliest deadline for the CSO Initiatives Facility is in mid-May.

results.proposal_summary: The Agence Française de Développement (AFD) provides grants through the FICOL facility to support development projects implemented by French local authorities in partnership with local authorities in developing and emerging countries. Grants range from EUR 200,000 to EUR 1,500,000, covering up to 70% of project costs, with a project duration of 3 years. results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund is offering innovation grants for climate adaptation projects, with proposals being reviewed on a rolling basis. The proposals under review are for projects in Nigeria, Thailand, Cuba, Uruguay, Libyan Arab Jamahiriya, Costa Rica, Honduras, Somalia and Togo, with funding amounts ranging from USD 0 to 15,000,000, with a public comment deadline of February 16, 2026. Eligibility is limited to implementing entities. Several deadlines have already passed.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on climate adaptation and resilience through nature-based solutions and community adaptation aligns with ILO's work on just transitions and environmentally sustainable economies.

results.exclusion_reason: This page lists completed projects. No active call for proposals is present; therefore, the ILO is ineligible.
results.classification_summary: The Adaptation Fund's "Projects to Date" page provides a comprehensive list of projects funded by the Adaptation Fund across various countries and regions. The information is categorized by funding type, including NIE Small Grants for Innovation, Adaptation Fund Climate Innovation Accelerator (AFCIA), Large Innovation Projects, and Innovation and learning bundled projects. Each project entry includes the country, title, implementing entity, approved amount, approved date, and status. Since the page does not contain an active call for proposals, the ILO is ineligible.

results.title: Key Materials – Adaptation Fund
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund's 'Key Materials' page serves as a central repository for information and links related to various funding opportunities focused on climate change adaptation. It provides access to resources such as training materials, publications, and news releases. However, the page does not contain a specific call for proposals with defined eligibility criteria, budget, or deadlines. Consequently, it is not possible to determine if the ILO is eligible or to extract the necessary information for a complete analysis.

results.exclusion_reason: The ILO is an international organization and is not explicitly listed as an eligible applicant in the provided document. The provided text concerns readiness grants targeting national implementing entities (NIEs).
results.classification_summary: This is a call for readiness grants from the Adaptation Fund. These grants target National Implementing Entities (NIEs) to support climate finance readiness activities, project formulation, scaling up projects, and technical assistance related to environmental, social, and gender policies.  The various grant types detailed include Readiness Package Grants (up to US$150,000 for NIE accreditation), Project Formulation Assistance (PFA) Grants, Project Scale-up Grants (up to US$100,000), and Technical Assistance (TA) Grants (up to US$25,000). The funding aims to build capacity in developing countries for adaptation projects. The ILO is not eligible.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The provided text concerns readiness grants targeting national implementing entities (NIEs), and does not list international organizations like the ILO as eligible applicants.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The description explicitly mentions 'grants' for various readiness activities, indicating a non-reimbursable component.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The Project Formulation Grants (PFG) are available to NIEs for up to USD 50,000.

Deadline is future.evidence: The document does not mention specific deadlines for applications. Because of this, I cannot evaluate this criteria.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The call is for grants to support capacity building and project development, not for procurement of goods or services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The document does not mention any co-financing requirements.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: The call is targeting NIEs, but doesn't require accreditation with another body besides the Adaptation Fund.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The document does not specify any consortium leadership rules.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: Eligibility is geared towards national implementing entities, so applicant origin is limited to countries with such entities who are eligible to apply.

results.title: Locally Led Adaptation
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund provides funding for Locally Led Adaptation (LLA) projects and programs, focusing on devolving decision-making to local levels and addressing inequalities. The fund supports various modalities for channeling grants to non-accredited entities and enhanced direct access. The initiative promotes climate change adaptation actions, with funding available for projects in various sectors. The Adaptation Fund Board adopted a medium-term strategy emphasizing LLA. There is no deadline given. The ILO is not eligible to apply.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on just transitions towards environmentally sustainable economies and societies aligns with ILO's mandate.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO is not an eligible entity as per the listed applicant types (NIEs, RIEs, and MIEs).
results.classification_summary: The Adaptation Fund is offering single-country grants up to USD 5 million to accredited National Implementing Entities (NIEs), Regional Implementing Entities (RIEs), and Multilateral Implementing Entities (MIEs) for locally-led adaptation projects. The call aims to devolve decision-making to national and sub-national levels in developing countries. Project Formulation Grants (PFG) are also available. The ILO is not an eligible applicant.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The call is open to accredited national implementing entities (NIEs), regional implementing entities (RIEs) and multilateral implementing entities (MIEs) of the Adaptation Fund. ILO is not explicitly listed.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call is for grants to implement projects.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The funding available is up to US$ 5 million.

Deadline is future.evidence: The document does not specify a deadline.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The call is for project proposals, not for procurement of goods or services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The document does not mention mandatory co-financing.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: MIEs, NIEs and RIEs applying for Single Country LLA grants must have an accreditation status of “Accredited” with the AF.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The document does not specify consortium leadership rules.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The call is open to developing country recipients.

results.title: LLA Single Country Grants
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund offers single-country grants up to USD 5 million to accredited National, Regional, and Multilateral Implementing Entities for locally-led adaptation projects in developing countries. The objective is to devolve decision-making in climate finance programming to national and sub-national levels. Project Formulation Grants are available. The deadline is unspecified. Co-financing is not mandatory. Eligible applicants must be accredited AF implementing entities.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on climate resilience and adaptation aligns with ILO's work on just transitions and creating environmentally sustainable economies and societies.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO is not an eligible entity based on eligibility criteria described in the text.
results.classification_summary: This is a call for proposals for Regional Locally Led Adaptation (LLA) grants from the Adaptation Fund. The grants are available to accredited regional implementing entities (RIEs) and multilateral implementing entities (MIEs) of the Adaptation Fund. The objective is to empower developing countries in climate finance. The funding available is up to US$ 30 million per project/programme. The submission process can be one-step, two-step, or three-step. The proposal should be sent to submissions@adaptation-fund.org. The ILO is not an eligible applicant.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The call is open to accredited regional implementing entities (RIEs) and multilateral implementing entities (MIEs) of the Adaptation Fund. ILO, as an international organization, is not explicitly listed as an eligible entity.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The funding is described as 'grants' for regional LLA projects, indicating a non-reimbursable component.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: Funding available is up to US$ 30 million per project/programme, which is above the US$ 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The document mentions funding between July 1, 2025, and June 30, 2026 (fiscal year). However, there is no specific deadline mentioned for proposal submission.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The call is for project proposals for locally-led adaptation, not for procurement of goods or services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The document does not explicitly state that co-financing is a mandatory requirement.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: RIEs and MIEs applying for Regional LLA grants must have an accreditation status of “Accredited” with the AF.

results.title: Approved LLA Single Country Grants – Adaptation Fund
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund provides grants for Locally Led Adaptation (LLA) Single Country projects. This page lists previously approved projects in countries like Cote d'Ivoire, Armenia, Honduras, Rwanda, Peru, and Belize, with funding amounts ranging from approximately $4 million to $5 million. The projects focus on enhancing resilience and adaptive capacity in various sectors. No application details are provided, as this page only presents already-approved projects.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The adaptation fund projects focus on improving climate resilience which indirectly improves working conditions in affected sectors, particularly agriculture, with interventions at the community level aligning with ILO's goal of decent work.

results.exclusion_reason: No specific call for proposals is listed on this page, only projects under review. Therefore, no eligibility criteria or deadlines are available.

results.title: LLA Proposals - Adaptation Fund (Projects Under Review)
results.deadline_text: Please sent your comments as early as possible and no later than August 10, 2025.
theme: Just Transitions towards Environmentally Sustainable Economies and Societies
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund's Locally Led Adaptation (LLA) program proposals are under review for single country, regional, and aggregator projects with the aim of promoting climate resilience. These proposals, submitted on a rolling basis, are listed on the Adaptation Fund website. The list includes projects in countries such as Sri Lanka, Mongolia, Belize and regions such as Southern Africa and the Caribbean. No information on specific grant amounts or deadlines is provided, as the proposals are already under review. Eligibility details are unavailable. No co-financing requirements are mentioned.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The focus on climate resilience and adaptation projects, particularly those that are locally led, aligns with the ILO's mandate in promoting just transitions and decent work in the context of environmental sustainability.

results.exclusion_reason: The Adaptation Fund is calling for proposals from National Implementing Entities (NIEs). The ILO is not an NIE, so it is ineligible.
results.classification_summary: The Adaptation Fund's Learning Grants program provides grants up to USD 500,000 to National Implementing Entities (NIEs) for knowledge management and sharing activities related to climate adaptation. These grants support activities such as lessons learned studies, technical guidance, partnerships, and knowledge platforms. The application process involves a technical review by the Adaptation Fund Board secretariat and review by the Project and Programme Review Committee. Proposals are accepted on a rolling basis with deadlines announced on their website.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The call is open to accredited National Implementing Entities (NIEs) of the Adaptation Fund. The ILO is not a NIE.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call is for learning grants, which implies a non-reimbursable component.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The maximum grant size is USD 500,000, which is above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The application is on a rolling basis, but we can only consider opportunities with fixed deadlines. Since there is no defined upcoming deadline, this is set to false.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The call is for learning grants to share knowledge, not for procurement.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The document does not explicitly state that co-financing is required.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: Applicants must be an accredited national implementing entity of the Adaptation Fund.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The provided text contains no information regarding consortium leadership rules.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The call is open to accredited National Implementing Entities, not restricted by country of origin within those accredited.

results.title: Learning Grants
results.deadline_text: The Adaptation Fund Board accepts and considers project proposals for Learning Grants on a rolling bases.
eligible_activities: Activities related to the monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) process
Activities related to encouraging innovation in adaptation
Activities that support catalyzing the locally-led initiatives and actors in research and academia
results.proposal_summary: The Adaptation Fund offers Learning Grants of up to USD 500,000 to accredited National Implementing Entities (NIEs). These grants aim to encourage a culture of learning and build NIE capacities in knowledge management related to climate adaptation interventions. Eligible activities include knowledge transfer, development of guidelines, studies, workshops, and resource centers. The Adaptation Fund Board secretariat reviews proposals, and the Board makes the final decision. Proposals are accepted on a rolling basis; NIEs must have an active Adaptation Fund project reaching its midpoint to apply. No co-financing conditions are mentioned.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The Adaptation Fund focuses on climate change adaptation, this is outside the ILO's core mandate of labour and social issues, resulting in a null score.
